messages.author: Ababo
messages.text: Wij hebben gewijzigd naar narrow band, maar net zoals op 869.525 heeft mijn maat veel storingen. Het groene puntje op T-Deck pinkt continue. We hadden hiervoor al de freq gewijzigd naar 869.900. Daar had hij geen storingen. Zijn huis is dicht tegen een station en waarschijnlijk zijn zij het die dit veroorzaken. (edited)

messages.author: Gilles
messages.text: Hi everybody, just to let you know that we are going to start installing a few MC repeaters around Brussels city centre in the next months. They’ll be set in the EU868 Narrow preset to follow the path of our Flemish neighbours.
messages.content_html: Hi everybody, just to let you know that we are going to start installing a few MC repeaters around Brussels city centre in the next months. They’ll be set in the EU868 Narrow preset to follow the path of our Flemish neighbours.

messages.content_html: Hey Belgian community, we like to prepare for the future and looking into standardizing scopes. Have a look at this article and leave your feedback here in Discord: The scopes page: https://alexvanboxel.name/en/page/bemesh/ (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Lieven B
messages.text: Nice writeup, but I did not see a lot of discussion around it on the radiocommunicatie.be Discord. Regarding repeater flood adverts, I would advise to set flood.advert.interval to something higher than 12. The maximum value used to be 48 hours, and I set is to 47 on my repeaters to shift the hour every two days. But since more recent versions it can be set as high as 168 (7 days). So maybe my default value will be 167, te less flood the better?
messages.content_html: Nice writeup, but I did not see a lot of discussion around it on the radiocommunicatie.be Discord. Regarding repeater flood adverts, I would advise to set flood.advert.interval to something higher than 12. The maximum value used to be 48 hours, and I set is to 47 on my repeaters to shift the hour every two days. But since more recent versions it can be set as high as 168 (7 days). So maybe my default value will be 167, te less flood the better?

messages.author: Alex Van Boxel
messages.text: I like the shifting and I’ll adapt it to 47. In Belgium I don’t think it’s a problem yet to do to 7 days.
messages.content_html: I like the shifting and I’ll adapt it to 47. In Belgium I don’t think it’s a problem yet to do to 7 days.

The maximum value used to be 48 hours, and I set is to 47 on my repeaters to shift the hour every two days. But since more recent versions it can be set as high as 168 (7 days). So maybe my default value will be 167, te less flood the better?

messages.author: Gilles
messages.text: I suppose it is better to keep frequent adverts on region that aren’t functionally a mesh yet ? In BRU it is still little pockets there and there. I was planning to lower the adverts frequency of my repeaters as soon as they are in reach of other pockets.
messages.content_html: I suppose it is better to keep frequent adverts on region that aren’t functionally a mesh yet ? In BRU it is still little pockets there and there. I was planning to lower the adverts frequency of my repeaters as soon as they are in reach of other pockets.

messages.author: Lieven B
messages.text: What would be a valid reason for frequent adverts?
messages.content_html: What would be a valid reason for frequent adverts?

messages.author: Alex Van Boxel
messages.text: This was discussed in the radiocommunicatie forum, before posting it here. Well the scopes at least. I don’t see the issue with the local advert c as it doesn’t propagate. And like @Gilles mentioned, as long as we have lots of disconnected pockets why go over 47 hours for flood?
messages.content_html: This was discussed in the radiocommunicatie forum, before posting it here. Well the scopes at least. I don’t see the issue with the local advert c as it doesn’t propagate. And like @Gilles mentioned, as long as we have lots of disconnected pockets why go over 47 hours for flood?
messages.reply_html:
Lieven B
@Alex Van Boxel I like your page about the scopes, but can you point to any discussions about this on any forums? I am just curious if there is any consensus or discussions in Belgium about this. You posted "we like to prepare for the future and looking into standardizing scopes.". Who is 'we'?

messages.author: hlabs
messages.text: Hello everyone, I live in Belgium and discovering Meshcore these days. I was wondering: why does anyone seem to have settle for 868 MHz whereas 433 MHz even with the reduced power limit of the IBPT/ITU would be better for propagation...?
messages.content_html: Hello everyone, I live in Belgium and discovering Meshcore these days. I was wondering: why does anyone seem to have settle for 868 MHz whereas 433 MHz even with the reduced power limit of the IBPT/ITU would be better for propagation...?

messages.author: Gilles
messages.text: I asked myself the same question. My theory is that most of the LoRa chips in the market are build for 910-870Mhz because they can be sold in several markets while a 433Mhz could be sold only in one market. So all the coolest LoRa gadgets used chips that can accommodate most markets. Then you have 868 getting most nodes in Europe. I’m new to this so this is just my uninformed guess ! (edited)
messages.content_html: I asked myself the same question. My theory is that most of the LoRa chips in the market are build for 910-870Mhz because they can be sold in several markets while a 433Mhz could be sold only in one market. So all the coolest LoRa gadgets used chips that can accommodate most markets. Then you have 868 getting most nodes in Europe. I’m new to this so this is just my uninformed guess ! (edited)
messages.reply_html:
hlabs
Hello everyone, I live in Belgium and discovering Meshcore these days. I was wondering: why does anyone seem to have settle for 868 MHz whereas 433 MHz even with the reduced power limit of the IBPT/ITU would be better for propagation...?
